title: polypogon parvulus
intro: Polypogon parvulus, also known as the small hair grass or little meadow grass, is a species of grass native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It is a perennial plant with a creeping habit, often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and providing relief from respiratory problems, thanks to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Polypogon parvulus can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, with the herbal preparations often used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
